title: Domestic Violence Protection Order in Bangladesh: How to Apply
metaTitle: Domestic Violence Protection Order Bangladesh – How to Apply
metaDescription: Complete guide to domestic violence protection orders in Bangladesh under the Domestic Violence (Prevention and Protection) Act 2010 — who can apply, how to file, types of orders, and role of a family lawyer.
heroIntro: Domestic violence — whether physical, sexual, emotional, or economic — is a recognized legal harm in Bangladesh under the Domestic Violence (Prevention and Protection) Act 2010. Victims have the right to immediate legal protection through court orders that can remove the abuser from the home, restrict their movements, and provide financial support. If you or someone you know is in an abusive situation, this guide explains the legal steps available.
toc: The Domestic Violence Act 2010 in Bangladesh
Who is Protected Under the Act?
What Constitutes Domestic Violence Under Bangladesh Law?
How to Apply for a Protection Order
What Happens After a Protection Order is Granted?

sections.h2: The Domestic Violence Act 2010 in Bangladesh
sections.content:
The Domestic Violence (Prevention and Protection) Act 2010 is Bangladesh's primary legislation addressing domestic abuse. It provides civil remedies — protection orders, residence orders, and monetary relief — that are separate from (though can be combined with) criminal action. The Act is administered through designated courts, typically the Executive Magistrate or Family Court.

If you or a family member is in immediate danger, contact the police (999) or a family lawyer in Dhaka immediately. Legal protection is available quickly under this Act.

sections.h2: What Constitutes Domestic Violence Under Bangladesh Law?
sections.content:
The Act covers several forms of domestic violence:

  • Physical violence: Assault, battery, causing bodily harm, or threatening physical violence.
  • Sexual violence: Sexual assault or coercion within the domestic relationship.
  • Psychological/emotional violence: Harassment, intimidation, threats, controlling behaviour.
  • Economic violence: Depriving the victim of financial resources, forcing them from the matrimonial home, preventing employment.
  • Dowry-related violence: Harassment, threats, or violence related to dowry demands.

The courts can issue several types of orders under the Act:

  • Protection Order: Prohibits the respondent from committing any act of domestic violence, intimidating the victim, or contacting the victim in any manner.
  • Residence Order: Allows the victim to remain in the matrimonial home and orders the respondent to leave, or grants alternative accommodation.
  • Monetary Relief Order: Orders the respondent to pay compensation for medical expenses, loss of earnings, and other expenses arising from the violence.
  • Custody Order (Interim): Awards temporary custody of children to the victim during proceedings.

sections.h2: How to Apply for a Protection Order
sections.content:
The application process for a domestic violence protection order:

  1. File an application: Submit a written complaint to the Executive Magistrate (First Class) or the appropriate court in the judicial district where the victim resides or where the violence occurred.
  2. Medical evidence: Obtain a medical examination certificate documenting injuries — this is critical evidence.
  3. Supporting documents: Gather relevant documents — marriage certificate, photographs, written threats, medical reports, witness statements.
  4. Court hearing: The court will summon the respondent. In urgent cases, an interim order can be issued the same day without hearing the respondent first.
  5. Final order: After hearing both parties, the court passes a final protection order if the complaint is substantiated.

A Dhaka family lawyer can prepare and file the application and represent you in court — protecting you from having to face the abuser alone in court.

In cases of immediate danger, the court can issue an interim (emergency) protection order on the same day of filing — even without hearing the respondent. The respondent is served notice later and given a date to contest the order. This provides immediate legal protection to victims in acute danger.

Additionally, the police have the power to provide immediate assistance to DV victims and can accompany the victim to retrieve personal belongings from a shared residence.

Protection orders under the DV Act are civil remedies. Victims can also simultaneously file:

  • Criminal complaint under Sections 326, 323, 506 Penal Code for physical assault and threats.
  • Case under Nari O Shishu Nirjatan Daman Ain 2000 for dowry-related violence and sexual offences.
  • Case under Sections 4 and 6 of the Dowry Prohibition Act 1980 for dowry demands and related violence.

Filing parallel civil (protection order) and criminal (police case) proceedings provides maximum legal protection. Both tracks can be pursued simultaneously. Contact our Uttara family law office for a confidential consultation.

faqs.question: How quickly can I get a protection order in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: In urgent cases, an interim protection order can be issued the same day the application is filed, without the respondent being heard first. Regular final orders take several weeks after both parties are heard.

faqs.question: Can I remain in the matrimonial home after getting a protection order?
faqs.answer: Yes. A residence order under the DV Act 2010 can require the abuser to leave the matrimonial home and allow the victim to remain, even if the property belongs to the husband.

faqs.question: What is the punishment for violating a domestic violence protection order?
faqs.answer: Violation of a protection order is a criminal offence under the DV Act, punishable by imprisonment and/or fine. Repeat violations carry enhanced penalties.

faqs.question: Can men also apply for domestic violence protection orders?
faqs.answer: The DV Act 2010 is primarily oriented toward women and children as victims. However, protections may extend to any person in a domestic relationship who faces violence.

faqs.question: Do I need a lawyer to apply for a protection order?
faqs.answer: It is not legally required but strongly recommended. A family lawyer can ensure the application is properly drafted, evidence is organized, and you are protected from having to face the respondent alone in court.
